title: oxalis minuta
intro: Oxalis minuta, also known as the small wood-sorrel or dwarf wood sorrel, is a perennial herb that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to South Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Oxalis minuta include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve kidney function. Oxalis minuta can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
